description: Improve MCP server telemetry with a disableGeoip fix, posthog-node v5 upgrade, session tracking, and four new anonymous platform-level events (server_stopped, extension_connected, dispatch_timed_out) — all without collecting tool names, plugin names, arguments, or any user-identifiable data, in accordance with documented privacy promises.

userStories.title: Upgrade posthog-node to v5 and enforce disableGeoip
userStories.description: Bump posthog-node from ^4.18.0 to ^5.29.2 in platform/mcp-server/package.json and add disableGeoip: true to the PostHog client constructor options in telemetry.ts. The top-of-file comment claims 'no IP logging' but the code never enforces it; this fix makes the code match the promise.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/package.json has 'posthog-node': '^5.29.2' in dependencies
The PostHog constructor call in telemetry.ts includes disableGeoip: true in its options object alongside host, flushAt, and flushInterval
No other telemetry logic changes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/package.json (bump posthog-node version)
- platform/mcp-server/src/telemetry.ts (add disableGeoip: true)

Context:
Current posthog-node version is ^4.18.0, latest is 5.29.2. The dynamic import in telemetry.ts is:
  const { PostHog } = await import('posthog-node');
  client = new PostHog(POSTHOG_API_KEY, {
    host: POSTHOG_HOST,
    flushAt: 1,
    flushInterval: 0,
  });
PostHog Node.js v5 has the same PostHog class constructor API — new PostHog(key, opts), .capture(), .shutdown() are unchanged. posthog-node v5 still accepts disableGeoip: boolean in PostHogOptions.

Changes:
1. In package.json: change '"posthog-node": "^4.18.0"' to '"posthog-node": "^5.29.2"'
2. In telemetry.ts: add disableGeoip: true to the PostHog constructor options:
  client = new PostHog(POSTHOG_API_KEY, {
    host: POSTHOG_HOST,
    flushAt: 1,
    flushInterval: 0,
    disableGeoip: true,
  });
3. Run npm install in platform/mcp-server/ to update the lockfile

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any logic, event names, or other options
- Do NOT modify the test file in this story
- The existing test mock in telemetry.test.ts (vi.mock('posthog-node')) will still work because v5 has the same class shape
- After npm install, commit the updated package-lock.json

userStories.title: Add session_id to telemetry module and enhance server_started event
userStories.description: Generate a per-process session UUID in initTelemetry() and expose it via getSessionId(). Use it in server_started, and update that event to break out plugin counts: rename pluginCount to plugins_loaded and add plugins_failed, plugins_local, plugins_npm.
acceptanceCriteria: telemetry.ts exports getSessionId(): string that returns the session UUID generated during initTelemetry()
getSessionId() returns undefined-safe value (empty string or UUID) when called before initTelemetry()
session_id generated in initTelemetry() is a UUIDv4 and is different from the persistent telemetry-id file value
server_started event in index.ts no longer includes pluginCount field; it includes plugins_loaded, plugins_failed, plugins_local, plugins_npm, and session_id instead
plugins_local counts plugins with source === 'local', plugins_npm counts plugins with source === 'npm'
plugins_failed is state.registry.failures.length

userStories.title: Add peakConcurrentDispatches and hadExtensionConnection to ServerState
userStories.description: Add two tracking fields to ServerState: peakConcurrentDispatches (the high-water mark of simultaneous pending dispatches) and hadExtensionConnection (whether the extension ever connected this session). Update the peak in dispatchToExtension after each new dispatch is registered.
acceptanceCriteria: ServerState interface in state.ts has peakConcurrentDispatches: number field
ServerState interface in state.ts has hadExtensionConnection: boolean field
createState() initializes peakConcurrentDispatches to 0 and hadExtensionConnection to false
STATE_SCHEMA_VERSION is bumped from 7 to 8

In dispatchToExtension in extension-protocol.ts, after state.pendingDispatches.set(id, pending), the peak is updated: if (state.pendingDispatches.size > state.peakConcurrentDispatches) state.peakConcurrentDispatches = state.pendingDispatches.size
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts (add fields to ServerState, update createState, bump STATE_SCHEMA_VERSION)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts (update peak after pendingDispatches.set)

Context:
ServerState interface is at line 277 in state.ts. STATE_SCHEMA_VERSION = 7 is at line 355.
createState() returns the default object at line 369.

The bump from 7 to 8 triggers a warning on hot reload ('State schema version changed') which prompts developers to restart the dev server — this is expected and correct.

The pending dispatch peak update goes in dispatchToExtension() in extension-protocol.ts.
The relevant code in dispatchToExtension (lines 325-347):
  const pending: PendingDispatch = {
    resolve,
    reject,
    label: dispatchLabel,
    startTs: Date.now(),
    timerId,
    progressToken: opts.progressToken,
    onProgress: opts.onProgress,
    connectionId: conn.connectionId,
  };
  state.pendingDispatches.set(id, pending);  // ← add peak update AFTER this line

Add immediately after state.pendingDispatches.set(id, pending):
  const currentSize = state.pendingDispatches.size;
  if (currentSize > state.peakConcurrentDispatches) {
    state.peakConcurrentDispatches = currentSize;
  }

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify dispatchToAllConnections — it has its own pending dispatch management
- hadExtensionConnection is NOT set in this story — it will be set in US-005 when the extension connects
- Do NOT remove or rename any existing ServerState fields
- The schema version bump is intentional; do not skip it

userStories.title: Emit server_stopped telemetry event at graceful shutdown
userStories.description: Add two pure helper functions to telemetry.ts (computeErrorRateBucket and classifyLoadFailures) and emit a server_stopped event in shutdown.ts before shutdownTelemetry() is called, capturing session duration, tool call counts, error rate, extension connectivity, peak concurrency, and plugin load failure breakdown.
acceptanceCriteria: telemetry.ts exports computeErrorRateBucket(total: number, errors: number): string that returns '0%' when errors=0, '<5%' when rate < 0.05, '<25%' when rate < 0.25, '>=25%' otherwise
computeErrorRateBucket(0, 0) returns '0%' (no division by zero)

telemetry.ts exports classifyLoadFailures that takes a readonly array of { path: string; error: string } and returns { missing_adapter: number, invalid_manifest: number, schema_error: number, unknown: number }
shutdown.ts emits trackEvent('server_stopped', ...) immediately before void shutdownTelemetry()
server_stopped event includes session_id, uptime_seconds, tool_calls_total, tool_errors_total, tool_error_rate_bucket, extension_was_connected, peak_concurrent_dispatches, plugin_load_failures

userStories.title: Emit extension_connected telemetry event on first extension connection
userStories.description: In createHandleWsOpen in http-routes.ts, emit an extension_connected event the first time any extension WebSocket connects this session (i.e., when state.hadExtensionConnection is false). Set hadExtensionConnection to true immediately after to prevent re-emission on reconnects.
acceptanceCriteria: extension_connected event is emitted exactly once per session — on the first extension WebSocket connection
Reconnects (same connectionId or new connectionId after first) do NOT emit extension_connected
extension_connected includes session_id, time_to_connect_bucket, and profile_count
time_to_connect_bucket is '<10s' for elapsed < 10s, '10-60s' for elapsed < 60s, '1-5min' for elapsed < 300s, '>5min' otherwise
profile_count is state.extensionConnections.size after adding the new connection, capped at 3

userStories.title: Emit dispatch_timed_out telemetry event on tool dispatch timeout
userStories.description: In the setTimeout callback inside dispatchToExtension in extension-protocol.ts, emit a dispatch_timed_out event before rejecting the pending promise. The event includes whether the dispatch received any progress updates before timing out and a bucket of how long it ran.
acceptanceCriteria: dispatch_timed_out event is emitted when a pending dispatch times out
had_progress_updates is true if the pending dispatch received at least one progress notification (lastProgressTs is set), false otherwise
duration_bucket is based on actual elapsed time (Date.now() - pending.startTs) not on timeoutMs: '<32s' maps to '30s', '<65s' to '1min', '<130s' to '2min', otherwise '5min'
Event includes session_id
dispatch_timed_out is NOT emitted when dispatch is rejected for other reasons (WebSocket send failure, extension disconnect)

userStories.title: Add unit tests for new telemetry features
userStories.description: Extend platform/mcp-server/src/telemetry.test.ts with tests covering: disableGeoip in PostHog init, getSessionId behavior, computeErrorRateBucket bucketing logic, and classifyLoadFailures categorization.
acceptanceCriteria: Test verifies disableGeoip: true is passed to PostHog constructor during initTelemetry
Test verifies getSessionId() returns a UUIDv4 after initTelemetry()
Test verifies getSessionId() returns '' (empty string) before initTelemetry()
Test verifies two calls to getSessionId() within the same module instance return the same value
Test verifies session_id from getSessionId() differs from the persistent telemetry-id file value
Test verifies computeErrorRateBucket(0, 0) returns '0%'
Test verifies computeErrorRateBucket(100, 0) returns '0%'
Test verifies computeErrorRateBucket(100, 1) returns '<5%'
Test verifies computeErrorRateBucket(100, 10) returns '<25%'
Test verifies computeErrorRateBucket(100, 50) returns '>=25%'
Test verifies classifyLoadFailures correctly counts missing_adapter, invalid_manifest, schema_error, and unknown categories
